1 # BDD Scenarios 2 3 ## Meta 4 - Design Reference: `.artifacts/current/design.md` 5 - Generated: 2026-04-03 6 - Discovery Method: Three Amigos (Agent Teams) 7 8 --- 9 10 ## Feature: SEC Filing Download & Caching 11 12 ### Context 13 Pipeline downloads 10-K filings from SEC via edgartools, preprocesses HTML, converts to Markdown, and caches locally. Supports batch pre-load (script) and just-in-time (agent tool) modes with different error handling strategies. 14 15 ### Rule: Pipeline checks cache before downloading 16 17 #### S-dl-01: Cache determines whether SEC is contacted 18 > Verifies cache-first behavior: cached filings skip download; cache misses trigger full pipeline; omitting fiscal_year requires SEC resolution first 19 20 - **Given** the filing store state for `` 10-K `` is `` 21 - **When** `process("", "10-K", )` is called 22 - **Then** `` 23 24 | ticker | fiscal_year | fiscal_year_arg | cache_state | expected_behavior | notes | 25 |--------|-------------|-----------------|-------------|-------------------|-------| 26 | NVDA | 2024 | fiscal_year=2024 | cached | Returns cached ParsedFiling; SECDownloader not called | explicit year, cache hit | 27 | AAPL | 2023 | fiscal_year=2023 | not cached | Downloads from SEC, preprocesses, converts, saves, returns ParsedFiling | explicit year, cache miss | 28 | NVDA | 2024 | omitted | cached | Contacts edgartools to resolve latest year (2024), then returns cached filing | omitted year requires SEC metadata call before cache check | 29 | TSLA | 2024 | omitted | not cached | Contacts edgartools to resolve latest year, then downloads, processes, saves | omitted year, full pipeline | 30 31 Category: Illustrative (table-driven) 32 Origin: Multiple (PO seeded, Dev challenged fiscal_year omission, QA challenged resolution side effect) 33 34 ### Rule: Batch returns per-ticker structured results 35 36 #### S-dl-02: Batch processes multiple tickers with mixed outcomes 37 > Verifies batch returns a Dict[str, Result] where each ticker has its own status, ParsedFiling, and error info 38 39 - **Given** `process_batch(["NVDA", "AAPL", "FAKECORP"], "10-K")` where NVDA is cached, AAPL is not cached, and FAKECORP has no SEC filings 40 - **When** the batch completes 41 - **Then** the result is a Dict with 3 entries: NVDA (success, from cache), AAPL (success, freshly downloaded), FAKECORP (failure, "no 10-K filings found") 42 - **And** NVDA and AAPL have valid ParsedFiling objects; FAKECORP has an error with actionable message 43 44 Category: Illustrative 45 Origin: Multiple (PO seeded, Dev challenged partial failure, QA challenged result contract) 46 47 ### Rule: Fiscal year resolves correctly for all company types 48 49 #### S-dl-03: Non-calendar fiscal year companies store with correct fiscal_year 50 > Verifies that companies with non-calendar fiscal years have the correct fiscal_year in the storage path and metadata 51 52 - **Given** `` has a fiscal year ending `` and edgartools reports fiscal_year as `` 53 - **When** `process("", "10-K")` completes 54 - **Then** the filing is stored at `data/sec_filings//10-K/.md` with `fiscal_year: ` in frontmatter 55 56 | ticker | fy_end_date | edgartools_fy | notes | 57 |--------|-------------|---------------|-------| 58 | NVDA | 2025-01-26 | 2025 | FY ends January; edgartools convention followed | 59 | MSFT | 2024-06-30 | 2024 | FY ends June | 60 | AAPL | 2024-09-28 | 2024 | FY ends September | 61 62 Category: Illustrative (table-driven) 63 Origin: Dev (fiscal year extraction reliability) 64 65 #### S-dl-04: "Latest" returns most recently filed 10-K 66 > Verifies that omitting fiscal_year returns the latest available filing, not a future unfiled one 67 68 - **Given** today is January 15, 2025, and NVDA's most recent filed 10-K is for FY2024 (filed Feb 2024), with FY2025 10-K not yet filed 69 - **When** `process("NVDA", "10-K")` is called without fiscal_year 70 - **Then** the pipeline returns the FY2024 filing (the latest actually filed), not an error about FY2025 71 72 Category: Illustrative 73 Origin: Dev (fiscal year boundary ambiguity) 74 75 ### Rule: Pipeline rejects invalid inputs with actionable errors 76 77 #### S-dl-05: Invalid inputs produce distinct, actionable error types 78 > Verifies that different error conditions produce distinguishable errors so callers (agent tools) can respond appropriately 79 80 - **Given** `` 81 - **When** `process("", "", )` is called 82 - **Then** the pipeline raises `` with a message containing `` 83 84 | input_condition | ticker | filing_type | fiscal_year | error_type | key_info | notes | 85 |-----------------|--------|-------------|-------------|------------|----------|-------| 86 | Ticker not in SEC | NVDAA | 10-K | omitted | TickerNotFoundError | "NVDAA" | typo, not a real ticker | 87 | Valid ticker, no 10-K (foreign filer) | TSM | 10-K | omitted | FilingNotFoundError | "TSM", "10-K" | TSM files 20-F, not 10-K | 88 | Valid ticker, FY doesn't exist | NVDA | 10-K | 1985 | FilingNotFoundError | "NVDA", "1985" | predates SEC filings | 89 | Unsupported filing type | AAPL | 10-Q | omitted | UnsupportedFilingTypeError | "10-Q", supported types | 10-Q not in FilingType enum | 90 91 Category: Illustrative (table-driven) 92 Origin: Multiple (Dev: error distinction + FilingType enforcement, QA: TSM + FY1985) 93 94 ### Rule: Ticker case is normalized to uppercase 95 96 #### S-dl-06: Lowercase and uppercase tickers produce identical results 97 > Verifies that ticker normalization happens at the pipeline entry point, preventing duplicate directories 98 99 - **Given** NVDA 10-K FY2024 was previously processed via `process("NVDA", "10-K", 2024)` 100 - **When** `process("nvda", "10-K", 2024)` is called with lowercase ticker 101 - **Then** the pipeline returns the cached filing from `data/sec_filings/NVDA/10-K/2024.md` (uppercase path) 102 - **And** no duplicate directory `data/sec_filings/nvda/` is created 103 104 Category: Illustrative 105 Origin: QA (ticker case normalization), Dev (store-level normalization) 106 107 ### Rule: Concurrent batch and JIT writes produce valid output 108 109 #### S-dl-07: Overlapping writes to same filing produce a complete, valid file 110 > Verifies that when batch and JIT target the same ticker/year simultaneously, the stored file is never corrupted 111 112 - **Given** no cached file exists for AAPL 10-K FY2024 113 - **When** a batch script processing AAPL and an agent JIT call for AAPL run concurrently 114 - **Then** the file at `data/sec_filings/AAPL/10-K/2024.md` is a complete, valid ParsedFiling (not truncated or interleaved) 115 - **And** `store.get("AAPL", FilingType.TEN_K, 2024)` returns parseable content 116 117 Category: Illustrative 118 Origin: Dev (race condition, contested and upgraded), QA (batch+JIT overlap) 119 120 ### Rule: Transient and permanent SEC errors are distinguishable 121 122 > **Note**: The retry mechanism itself (batch silent retry vs JIT fail-fast, exponential backoff, retry count) is tested at the unit test level via mocks, since SEC error responses cannot be controlled in integration tests. The BDD scenario below verifies the observable behavior with real SEC calls: that different error conditions produce distinguishable error types so callers can decide whether to retry. 123 124 *See S-dl-05 for the full error type distinction table, which covers both permanent errors (ticker not found, no 10-K, non-existent FY) and unsupported filing types — all testable with real SEC calls.* 125 126 ### Rule: Cache recovery via force re-process 127 128 #### S-dl-08: force=True bypasses cache and re-processes 129 > Verifies that callers can force re-processing of a previously cached filing 130 131 - **Given** NVDA 10-K FY2024 is cached with `converter: markdownify` (from a previous fallback) 132 - **When** `process("NVDA", "10-K", fiscal_year=2024, force=True)` is called after html-to-markdown is now available 133 - **Then** the pipeline re-downloads, re-preprocesses, and re-converts the filing 134 - **And** the cached file is overwritten with `converter: html-to-markdown` and updated `parsed_at` 135 136 Category: Illustrative 137 Origin: Dev (save overwrite semantics), QA (bad cached content persists), User decision 138 139 --- 140 141 ### Journey Scenarios 142 143 #### J-dl-01: Batch pre-load followed by cache-only re-run 144 > Proves batch pre-load caches all filings and subsequent runs avoid SEC downloads entirely 145 146 - **Given** no filings are cached for NVDA, AAPL, or TSLA 147 - **When** a batch script runs `process_batch(["NVDA", "AAPL", "TSLA"], "10-K")` and all three download successfully 148 - **And** the same batch is run again immediately after 149 - **Then** the first run produces 3 successful entries in the result dict (all freshly downloaded) 150 - **And** the second run produces 3 successful entries (all from cache, zero SEC downloads) 151 152 Category: Journey 153 Origin: Multiple 154 155 #### J-dl-02: Agent JIT cache miss triggers download, then cache hit on follow-up 156 > Proves the JIT flow: agent detects cache miss, triggers pipeline, then subsequent request is served from cache 157 158 - **Given** no filing exists for MSFT 10-K in the store 159 - **When** an agent calls `process("MSFT", "10-K")` (JIT, fiscal_year omitted) 160 - **And** the pipeline resolves latest fiscal year, downloads, preprocesses, converts, and saves 161 - **And** the agent later calls `process("MSFT", "10-K")` again for a follow-up question 162 - **Then** the first call returns a freshly processed ParsedFiling with correct metadata 163 - **And** the second call returns the same filing from cache without contacting SEC for the download 164 165 Category: Journey 166 Origin: Multiple 167 168 #### J-dl-03: Force re-process corrects bad cached output 169 > Proves the cache recovery flow: bad output is detected, force re-processes, and produces corrected output 170 171 - **Given** NVDA 10-K FY2024 was previously cached but the Markdown body is empty (converter silent failure at the time) 172 - **When** a user detects the issue during manual inspection 173 - **And** calls `process("NVDA", "10-K", fiscal_year=2024, force=True)` 174 - **Then** the pipeline re-downloads and re-processes the filing 175 - **And** the cached file is replaced with a valid ParsedFiling containing Markdown with heading structure 176 177 Category: Journey 178 Origin: Multiple (Dev: overwrite semantics, QA: bad cached content, User: force=True) 179 180 --- 181 182 ## Feature: HTML Preprocessing 183 184 ### Context 185 Preprocessor cleans SEC-specific HTML noise (XBRL inline tags, decorative styles, hidden elements) while preserving content and structural signals needed by the Markdown converter. The preprocessor must be aware that downstream heading detection depends on certain style attributes. 186 187 ### Rule: XBRL tags are unwrapped, preserving all inner content 188 189 #### S-prep-01: Nested XBRL tags stripped with all text content preserved 190 > Verifies that XBRL inline tags are unwrapped (children kept) regardless of nesting depth 191 192 - **Given** HTML containing `` 193 - **When** the preprocessor runs 194 - **Then** the output contains `` 195 196 | xbrl_pattern | expected_output | notes | 197 |--------------|-----------------|-------| 198 | `47525000000` | `47525000000` | simple number wrapping | 199 | `

Revenue was 47525 million

` | `

Revenue was 47525 million

` | nested XBRL, both layers unwrapped | 200 | `

Item 1: Business

NVIDIA designs GPUs...

` | `

Item 1: Business

NVIDIA designs GPUs...

` | section-wrapping XBRL, children preserved | 201 202 Category: Illustrative (table-driven) 203 Origin: Multiple (PO seeded, Dev challenged nesting, QA challenged section-wrapping) 204 205 ### Rule: Decorative styles removed, semantic styles preserved 206 207 #### S-prep-02: Style stripping distinguishes decorative from structural 208 > Verifies that purely decorative CSS properties are removed while layout-meaningful ones are preserved 209 210 - **Given** HTML containing `` 211 - **When** the preprocessor runs 212 - **Then** the output is `` 213 214 | element | expected | notes | 215 |---------|----------|-------| 216 | `

Revenue grew 125%

` | `

Revenue grew 125%

` | decorative styles stripped, text preserved | 217 | `$47,525` | `$47,525` | text-align preserved for table formatting | 218 | `
SEC metadata block
` | *(removed entirely)* | hidden element removed with all children | 219 220 Category: Illustrative (table-driven) 221 Origin: Multiple (PO seeded, Dev challenged legitimate styles, QA challenged table alignment) 222 223 ### Rule: Content-bearing elements are unwrapped, not deleted 224 225 #### S-prep-03: Older filings with font-tag content are not emptied by preprocessing 226 > Verifies that font/span tags carrying visible content are unwrapped (tag removed, children kept), not deleted 227 228 - **Given** a pre-2010 filing where the document body contains `Item 1: BusinessNVIDIA designs and sells GPUs for gaming and data centers...` 229 - **When** the preprocessor runs 230 - **Then** the output preserves all text content: `Item 1: BusinessNVIDIA designs and sells GPUs for gaming and data centers...` 231 - **And** the output is NOT empty 232 233 Category: Illustrative 234 Origin: Multiple (Dev: unwrap vs remove, QA: empty document after aggressive preprocessing) 235 236 ### Rule: EDGAR boilerplate removal [UNCERTAIN — deferred to Implementation Planning] 237 238 > This rule's necessity depends on whether edgartools returns raw filing HTML (without EDGAR viewer chrome) or the full EDGAR viewer page. To be verified during Implementation Planning by actually fetching sample filings. If edgartools returns raw filing HTML only, this rule is unnecessary and should be removed. 239 240 --- 241 242 ### Journey Scenarios 243 244 #### J-prep-01: Real filing HTML through full preprocess produces intact content 245 > Proves the preprocessor preserves all meaningful content through the full cleaning pipeline 246 247 - **Given** a real 10-K filing HTML for NVDA FY2024 (containing XBRL inline tags, inline styles, and hidden metadata elements) 248 - **When** the full preprocessing pipeline runs (all cleaning rules applied sequentially) 249 - **Then** the output HTML contains all visible text content from the original 250 - **And** no XBRL namespace tags remain 251 - **And** the output is not empty or significantly shorter than the original visible text 252 253 Category: Journey 254 Origin: Multiple 255 256 --- 257 258 ## Feature: Markdown Conversion & Output Quality 259 260 ### Context 261 Converter transforms preprocessed HTML to Markdown, preserving heading hierarchy for downstream RAG chunking. Two adapters available: html-to-markdown (Rust, primary) and markdownify (Python, fallback). Both must produce ATX-style headings for consistency. 262 263 ### Rule: Heading hierarchy is preserved in output 264 265 #### S-conv-01: Filings with semantic HTML headings convert to ATX Markdown headings 266 > Verifies that standard h1-h6 tags become ATX-style Markdown headings in correct order 267 268 - **Given** preprocessed HTML containing `

Item 1: Business

` followed by `

Item 1A: Risk Factors

` followed by `

Market Competition

` 269 - **When** the converter processes the HTML 270 - **Then** the Markdown output contains `## Item 1: Business`, `## Item 1A: Risk Factors`, and `### Market Competition` in that order 271 272 Category: Illustrative 273 Origin: PO 274 275 #### S-conv-02: Filings with styled-p headings still produce heading hierarchy 276 > Verifies end-to-end that the preprocessor preserves enough structural signal for the converter to identify headings in older filings 277 278 - **Given** a filing where section headers are `

ITEM 1. BUSINESS

` with no h1-h6 tags 279 - **When** the full pipeline runs (preprocess → convert) 280 - **Then** the Markdown output has "ITEM 1. BUSINESS" as a heading (ATX format), not as a plain paragraph 281 - **And** the document has at least one `#`/`##` heading in the output 282 283 Category: Illustrative 284 Origin: Multiple (Dev: styled-p headings, QA: no-heading filings, QA: preprocessor/converter ordering conflict) 285 286 ### Rule: Converter falls back gracefully at import-time and invocation-time 287 288 #### S-conv-03: Converter fallback triggers correctly 289 > Verifies that the pipeline falls back to markdownify under various failure conditions 290 291 - **Given** `` 292 - **When** a filing is processed through the conversion step 293 - **Then** `` and the frontmatter records `converter: ` 294 295 | failure_condition | expected_behavior | converter_used | notes | 296 |-------------------|-------------------|----------------|-------| 297 | html-to-markdown cannot be imported (linux-aarch64, no wheel) | markdownify used for all conversions from startup | markdownify | import-time fallback | 298 | html-to-markdown raises exception on specific HTML input | markdownify used for that filing only | markdownify | invocation-time fallback | 299 | html-to-markdown returns empty string or whitespace-only output | Detected as silent failure; markdownify used for that filing | markdownify | silent failure detection | 300 301 > **Note**: Fallback triggers on exception, empty output, or whitespace-only output. Ratio-based size thresholds (e.g., output < X% of input) are intentionally excluded — HTML preprocessing legitimately strips large amounts of XBRL/style noise, so a small output relative to raw HTML does not indicate converter failure. Content quality (e.g., missing sections) is validated by UAT manual inspection, not by automated fallback. 302 303 Category: Illustrative (table-driven) 304 Origin: Multiple (Dev: import vs invocation, Dev: silent failure, QA: threshold definition, User: Q3 decision) 305 306 ### Rule: Both converters produce ATX-style headings 307 308 #### S-conv-04: Same input produces ATX headings from both adapters 309 > Verifies that both html-to-markdown and markdownify produce `## Heading` format (not Setext `Heading\n------`) 310 311 - **Given** a preprocessed HTML snippet containing `

Item 7: Management's Discussion

` 312 - **When** the same HTML is converted by HtmlToMarkdownAdapter and by MarkdownifyAdapter separately 313 - **Then** both outputs contain `## Item 7: Management's Discussion` (ATX format) 314 - **And** neither output uses Setext-style headings 315 316 Category: Illustrative 317 Origin: Dev (converter normalization, contested and upgraded by User Q4 decision) 318 319 --- 320 321 ### Journey Scenarios 322 323 #### J-conv-01: Full pipeline with mixed content produces structured Markdown 324 > Proves the complete pipeline preserves document structure through all stages 325 326 - **Given** a real 10-K filing HTML containing headings, paragraphs, financial tables, and XBRL inline tags 327 - **When** the full pipeline runs: download → preprocess → convert → save 328 - **Then** the saved Markdown file has YAML frontmatter with all required fields 329 - **And** the Markdown body contains ATX headings reflecting the filing's section structure 330 - **And** financial tables are present as Markdown tables 331 - **And** no residual HTML tags or XBRL namespace tags remain in the body 332 333 Category: Journey 334 Origin: Multiple 335 336 --- 337 338 ## Feature: Filing Store & Output Contract 339 340 ### Context 341 LocalFilingStore persists ParsedFilings as .md files with YAML frontmatter at `data/sec_filings/{ticker}/{filing_type}/{fiscal_year}.md`. Store operations must be consistent, handle real-world filesystem conditions, and produce correctly typed metadata. 342 343 ### Rule: Store operations are mutually consistent 344 345 #### S-store-01: save/exists/get/list_filings are consistent with each other 346 > Verifies that all four store operations reflect the same state 347 348 - **Given** `` 349 - **When** `` is called 350 - **Then** `` 351 352 | precondition | operation | expected_result | notes | 353 |--------------|-----------|-----------------|-------| 354 | TSLA 10-K FY2024 saved | `exists("TSLA", FilingType.TEN_K, 2024)` | `True` | post-save existence | 355 | TSLA 10-K FY2024 saved | `get("TSLA", FilingType.TEN_K, 2024)` | Returns ParsedFiling with valid frontmatter | post-save retrieval | 356 | No TSLA FY2023 saved | `exists("TSLA", FilingType.TEN_K, 2023)` | `False` | non-existent filing | 357 | No TSLA FY2023 saved | `get("TSLA", FilingType.TEN_K, 2023)` | `None` | non-existent filing | 358 | NVDA FY2024, 2023, 2022 saved | `list_filings("NVDA", FilingType.TEN_K)` | Returns `[2024, 2023, 2022]` | list all fiscal years | 359 | No MSFT filings saved | `list_filings("MSFT", FilingType.TEN_K)` | Returns `[]` | empty list | 360 361 Category: Illustrative (table-driven) 362 Origin: PO 363 364 ### Rule: Store creates directories automatically on first save 365 366 #### S-store-02: First save for a new ticker creates the directory structure 367 > Verifies that save() creates intermediate directories when they don't exist 368 369 - **Given** `data/sec_filings/TSLA/` directory does not exist 370 - **When** `save()` is called for TSLA 10-K FY2024 371 - **Then** the directory `data/sec_filings/TSLA/10-K/` is created automatically 372 - **And** the file `data/sec_filings/TSLA/10-K/2024.md` is written successfully 373 374 Category: Illustrative 375 Origin: Dev (directory auto-creation) 376 377 ### Rule: list_filings filters non-filing files 378 379 #### S-store-03: Non-filing files in the directory are excluded from list results 380 > Verifies that filesystem artifacts don't pollute list_filings results 381 382 - **Given** `data/sec_filings/NVDA/10-K/` contains `2024.md`, `2023.md`, `.DS_Store`, and `2024.md.tmp` 383 - **When** `list_filings("NVDA", FilingType.TEN_K)` is called 384 - **Then** the result is `[2024, 2023]` — only valid `{year}.md` files are included 385 386 Category: Illustrative 387 Origin: QA (.DS_Store), QA (temp file orphans) 388 389 ### Rule: ParsedFiling metadata roundtrips correctly through save/get 390 391 #### S-store-04: Frontmatter with special characters survives save/get roundtrip 392 > Verifies that company names with YAML-problematic characters are serialized and deserialized correctly 393 394 - **Given** a ParsedFiling for `` with company_name `` is saved 395 - **When** `store.get("", FilingType.TEN_K, )` is called 396 - **Then** the returned ParsedFiling has `company_name` equal to `` (exact match) 397 398 | ticker | company_name | year | yaml_issue | 399 |--------|-------------|------|------------| 400 | MCO | Moody's Corporation | 2024 | apostrophe | 401 | T | AT&T Inc. | 2024 | ampersand | 402 | TROW | T. Rowe Price Group, Inc. | 2024 | comma and periods | 403 404 Category: Illustrative (table-driven) 405 Origin: Multiple (QA: YAML special chars, Dev: YAML serializer roundtrip) 406 407 #### S-store-05: All required metadata fields are present and correctly typed 408 > Verifies that a saved ParsedFiling has the complete frontmatter contract 409 410 - **Given** NVDA 10-K FY2024 has been processed and saved 411 - **When** `store.get("NVDA", FilingType.TEN_K, 2024)` is called 412 - **Then** the frontmatter contains all required fields: 413 - `ticker`: "NVDA" (string, uppercase) 414 - `cik`: string (e.g., "1045810") 415 - `company_name`: string (e.g., "NVIDIA Corporation") 416 - `filing_type`: "10-K" (string) 417 - `filing_date`: ISO date string 418 - `fiscal_year`: 2024 (integer) 419 - `accession_number`: string in dashed format (e.g., "0001045810-24-000029") 420 - `source_url`: valid URL string 421 - `parsed_at`: ISO 8601 UTC datetime with timezone (e.g., "2026-04-03T10:30:00Z") 422 - `converter`: "html-to-markdown" or "markdownify" (string) 423 424 Category: Illustrative 425 Origin: PO 426 427 --- 428 429 ### Journey Scenarios 430 431 #### J-store-01: Multi-ticker filing lifecycle through the store 432 > Proves store operations work correctly across multiple tickers and fiscal years 433 434 - **Given** a fresh (empty) filing store 435 - **When** filings are saved for NVDA FY2024, NVDA FY2023, and AAPL FY2024 436 - **And** `list_filings("NVDA", FilingType.TEN_K)` is called 437 - **And** `get("NVDA", FilingType.TEN_K, 2024)` is called 438 - **And** `get("AAPL", FilingType.TEN_K, 2024)` is called 439 - **Then** list_filings returns `[2024, 2023]` for NVDA 440 - **And** both get() calls return ParsedFilings with correct ticker-specific metadata 441 - **And** NVDA's filing is distinct from AAPL's (different ticker, cik, company_name) 442 443 Category: Journey 444 Origin: Multiple 445

# BDD Scenarios ## Meta - Design Reference: `.artifacts/current/design.md` - Generated: 2026-04-03 - Discovery Method: Three Amigos (Agent Teams) --- ## Feature: SEC Filing Download & Caching ### Context Pipeline downloads 10-K filings from SEC via edgartools, preprocesses HTML, converts to Markdown, and caches locally. Supports batch pre-load (script) and just-in-time (agent tool) modes with different error handling strategies. ### Rule: Pipeline checks cache before downloading #### S-dl-01: Cache determines whether SEC is contacted > Verifies cache-first behavior: cached filings skip download; cache misses trigger full pipeline; omitting fiscal_year requires SEC resolution first - **Given** the filing store state for `` 10-K `` is `` - **When** `process("", "10-K", )` is called - **Then** `` | ticker | fiscal_year | fiscal_year_arg | cache_state | expected_behavior | notes | |--------|-------------|-----------------|-------------|-------------------|-------| | NVDA | 2024 | fiscal_year=2024 | cached | Returns cached ParsedFiling; SECDownloader not called | explicit year, cache hit | | AAPL | 2023 | fiscal_year=2023 | not cached | Downloads from SEC, preprocesses, converts, saves, returns ParsedFiling | explicit year, cache miss | | NVDA | 2024 | omitted | cached | Contacts edgartools to resolve latest year (2024), then returns cached filing | omitted year requires SEC metadata call before cache check | | TSLA | 2024 | omitted | not cached | Contacts edgartools to resolve latest year, then downloads, processes, saves | omitted year, full pipeline | Category: Illustrative (table-driven) Origin: Multiple (PO seeded, Dev challenged fiscal_year omission, QA challenged resolution side effect) ### Rule: Batch returns per-ticker structured results #### S-dl-02: Batch processes multiple tickers with mixed outcomes > Verifies batch returns a Dict[str, Result] where each ticker has its own status, ParsedFiling, and error info - **Given** `process_batch(["NVDA", "AAPL", "FAKECORP"], "10-K")` where NVDA is cached, AAPL is not cached, and FAKECORP has no SEC filings - **When** the batch completes - **Then** the result is a Dict with 3 entries: NVDA (success, from cache), AAPL (success, freshly downloaded), FAKECORP (failure, "no 10-K filings found") - **And** NVDA and AAPL have valid ParsedFiling objects; FAKECORP has an error with actionable message Category: Illustrative Origin: Multiple (PO seeded, Dev challenged partial failure, QA challenged result contract) ### Rule: Fiscal year resolves correctly for all company types #### S-dl-03: Non-calendar fiscal year companies store with correct fiscal_year > Verifies that companies with non-calendar fiscal years have the correct fiscal_year in the storage path and metadata - **Given** `` has a fiscal year ending `` and edgartools reports fiscal_year as `` - **When** `process("", "10-K")` completes - **Then** the filing is stored at `data/sec_filings//10-K/.md` with `fiscal_year: ` in frontmatter | ticker | fy_end_date | edgartools_fy | notes | |--------|-------------|---------------|-------| | NVDA | 2025-01-26 | 2025 | FY ends January; edgartools convention followed | | MSFT | 2024-06-30 | 2024 | FY ends June | | AAPL | 2024-09-28 | 2024 | FY ends September | Category: Illustrative (table-driven) Origin: Dev (fiscal year extraction reliability) #### S-dl-04: "Latest" returns most recently filed 10-K > Verifies that omitting fiscal_year returns the latest available filing, not a future unfiled one - **Given** today is January 15, 2025, and NVDA's most recent filed 10-K is for FY2024 (filed Feb 2024), with FY2025 10-K not yet filed - **When** `process("NVDA", "10-K")` is called without fiscal_year - **Then** the pipeline returns the FY2024 filing (the latest actually filed), not an error about FY2025 Category: Illustrative Origin: Dev (fiscal year boundary ambiguity) ### Rule: Pipeline rejects invalid inputs with actionable errors #### S-dl-05: Invalid inputs produce distinct, actionable error types > Verifies that different error conditions produce distinguishable errors so callers (agent tools) can respond appropriately - **Given** `` - **When** `process("", "", )` is called - **Then** the pipeline raises `` with a message containing `` | input_condition | ticker | filing_type | fiscal_year | error_type | key_info | notes | |-----------------|--------|-------------|-------------|------------|----------|-------| | Ticker not in SEC | NVDAA | 10-K | omitted | TickerNotFoundError | "NVDAA" | typo, not a real ticker | | Valid ticker, no 10-K (foreign filer) | TSM | 10-K | omitted | FilingNotFoundError | "TSM", "10-K" | TSM files 20-F, not 10-K | | Valid ticker, FY doesn't exist | NVDA | 10-K | 1985 | FilingNotFoundError | "NVDA", "1985" | predates SEC filings | | Unsupported filing type | AAPL | 10-Q | omitted | UnsupportedFilingTypeError | "10-Q", supported types | 10-Q not in FilingType enum | Category: Illustrative (table-driven) Origin: Multiple (Dev: error distinction + FilingType enforcement, QA: TSM + FY1985) ### Rule: Ticker case is normalized to uppercase #### S-dl-06: Lowercase and uppercase tickers produce identical results > Verifies that ticker normalization happens at the pipeline entry point, preventing duplicate directories - **Given** NVDA 10-K FY2024 was previously processed via `process("NVDA", "10-K", 2024)` - **When** `process("nvda", "10-K", 2024)` is called with lowercase ticker - **Then** the pipeline returns the cached filing from `data/sec_filings/NVDA/10-K/2024.md` (uppercase path) - **And** no duplicate directory `data/sec_filings/nvda/` is created Category: Illustrative Origin: QA (ticker case normalization), Dev (store-level normalization) ### Rule: Concurrent batch and JIT writes produce valid output #### S-dl-07: Overlapping writes to same filing produce a complete, valid file > Verifies that when batch and JIT target the same ticker/year simultaneously, the stored file is never corrupted - **Given** no cached file exists for AAPL 10-K FY2024 - **When** a batch script processing AAPL and an agent JIT call for AAPL run concurrently - **Then** the file at `data/sec_filings/AAPL/10-K/2024.md` is a complete, valid ParsedFiling (not truncated or interleaved) - **And** `store.get("AAPL", FilingType.TEN_K, 2024)` returns parseable content Category: Illustrative Origin: Dev (race condition, contested and upgraded), QA (batch+JIT overlap) ### Rule: Transient and permanent SEC errors are distinguishable > **Note**: The retry mechanism itself (batch silent retry vs JIT fail-fast, exponential backoff, retry count) is tested at the unit test level via mocks, since SEC error responses cannot be controlled in integration tests. The BDD scenario below verifies the observable behavior with real SEC calls: that different error conditions produce distinguishable error types so callers can decide whether to retry. *See S-dl-05 for the full error type distinction table, which covers both permanent errors (ticker not found, no 10-K, non-existent FY) and unsupported filing types — all testable with real SEC calls.* ### Rule: Cache recovery via force re-process #### S-dl-08: force=True bypasses cache and re-processes > Verifies that callers can force re-processing of a previously cached filing - **Given** NVDA 10-K FY2024 is cached with `converter: markdownify` (from a previous fallback) - **When** `process("NVDA", "10-K", fiscal_year=2024, force=True)` is called after html-to-markdown is now available - **Then** the pipeline re-downloads, re-preprocesses, and re-converts the filing - **And** the cached file is overwritten with `converter: html-to-markdown` and updated `parsed_at` Category: Illustrative Origin: Dev (save overwrite semantics), QA (bad cached content persists), User decision --- ### Journey Scenarios #### J-dl-01: Batch pre-load followed by cache-only re-run > Proves batch pre-load caches all filings and subsequent runs avoid SEC downloads entirely - **Given** no filings are cached for NVDA, AAPL, or TSLA - **When** a batch script runs `process_batch(["NVDA", "AAPL", "TSLA"], "10-K")` and all three download successfully - **And** the same batch is run again immediately after - **Then** the first run produces 3 successful entries in the result dict (all freshly downloaded) - **And** the second run produces 3 successful entries (all from cache, zero SEC downloads) Category: Journey Origin: Multiple #### J-dl-02: Agent JIT cache miss triggers download, then cache hit on follow-up > Proves the JIT flow: agent detects cache miss, triggers pipeline, then subsequent request is served from cache - **Given** no filing exists for MSFT 10-K in the store - **When** an agent calls `process("MSFT", "10-K")` (JIT, fiscal_year omitted) - **And** the pipeline resolves latest fiscal year, downloads, preprocesses, converts, and saves - **And** the agent later calls `process("MSFT", "10-K")` again for a follow-up question - **Then** the first call returns a freshly processed ParsedFiling with correct metadata - **And** the second call returns the same filing from cache without contacting SEC for the download Category: Journey Origin: Multiple #### J-dl-03: Force re-process corrects bad cached output > Proves the cache recovery flow: bad output is detected, force re-processes, and produces corrected output - **Given** NVDA 10-K FY2024 was previously cached but the Markdown body is empty (converter silent failure at the time) - **When** a user detects the issue during manual inspection - **And** calls `process("NVDA", "10-K", fiscal_year=2024, force=True)` - **Then** the pipeline re-downloads and re-processes the filing - **And** the cached file is replaced with a valid ParsedFiling containing Markdown with heading structure Category: Journey Origin: Multiple (Dev: overwrite semantics, QA: bad cached content, User: force=True) --- ## Feature: HTML Preprocessing ### Context Preprocessor cleans SEC-specific HTML noise (XBRL inline tags, decorative styles, hidden elements) while preserving content and structural signals needed by the Markdown converter. The preprocessor must be aware that downstream heading detection depends on certain style attributes. ### Rule: XBRL tags are unwrapped, preserving all inner content #### S-prep-01: Nested XBRL tags stripped with all text content preserved > Verifies that XBRL inline tags are unwrapped (children kept) regardless of nesting depth - **Given** HTML containing `` - **When** the preprocessor runs - **Then** the output contains `` | xbrl_pattern | expected_output | notes | |--------------|-----------------|-------| | `47525000000` | `47525000000` | simple number wrapping | | `

Revenue was 47525 million

` | `

Revenue was 47525 million

` | nested XBRL, both layers unwrapped | | `

Item 1: Business

NVIDIA designs GPUs...

` | `

Item 1: Business

NVIDIA designs GPUs...

` | section-wrapping XBRL, children preserved | Category: Illustrative (table-driven) Origin: Multiple (PO seeded, Dev challenged nesting, QA challenged section-wrapping) ### Rule: Decorative styles removed, semantic styles preserved #### S-prep-02: Style stripping distinguishes decorative from structural > Verifies that purely decorative CSS properties are removed while layout-meaningful ones are preserved - **Given** HTML containing `` - **When** the preprocessor runs - **Then** the output is `` | element | expected | notes | |---------|----------|-------| | `

Revenue grew 125%

` | `

Revenue grew 125%

` | decorative styles stripped, text preserved | | `$47,525` | `$47,525` | text-align preserved for table formatting | | `
SEC metadata block
` | *(removed entirely)* | hidden element removed with all children | Category: Illustrative (table-driven) Origin: Multiple (PO seeded, Dev challenged legitimate styles, QA challenged table alignment) ### Rule: Content-bearing elements are unwrapped, not deleted #### S-prep-03: Older filings with font-tag content are not emptied by preprocessing > Verifies that font/span tags carrying visible content are unwrapped (tag removed, children kept), not deleted - **Given** a pre-2010 filing where the document body contains `Item 1: BusinessNVIDIA designs and sells GPUs for gaming and data centers...` - **When** the preprocessor runs - **Then** the output preserves all text content: `Item 1: BusinessNVIDIA designs and sells GPUs for gaming and data centers...` - **And** the output is NOT empty Category: Illustrative Origin: Multiple (Dev: unwrap vs remove, QA: empty document after aggressive preprocessing) ### Rule: EDGAR boilerplate removal [UNCERTAIN — deferred to Implementation Planning] > This rule's necessity depends on whether edgartools returns raw filing HTML (without EDGAR viewer chrome) or the full EDGAR viewer page. To be verified during Implementation Planning by actually fetching sample filings. If edgartools returns raw filing HTML only, this rule is unnecessary and should be removed. --- ### Journey Scenarios #### J-prep-01: Real filing HTML through full preprocess produces intact content > Proves the preprocessor preserves all meaningful content through the full cleaning pipeline - **Given** a real 10-K filing HTML for NVDA FY2024 (containing XBRL inline tags, inline styles, and hidden metadata elements) - **When** the full preprocessing pipeline runs (all cleaning rules applied sequentially) - **Then** the output HTML contains all visible text content from the original - **And** no XBRL namespace tags remain - **And** the output is not empty or significantly shorter than the original visible text Category: Journey Origin: Multiple --- ## Feature: Markdown Conversion & Output Quality ### Context Converter transforms preprocessed HTML to Markdown, preserving heading hierarchy for downstream RAG chunking. Two adapters available: html-to-markdown (Rust, primary) and markdownify (Python, fallback). Both must produce ATX-style headings for consistency. ### Rule: Heading hierarchy is preserved in output #### S-conv-01: Filings with semantic HTML headings convert to ATX Markdown headings > Verifies that standard h1-h6 tags become ATX-style Markdown headings in correct order - **Given** preprocessed HTML containing `

Item 1: Business

` followed by `

Item 1A: Risk Factors

` followed by `

Market Competition

` - **When** the converter processes the HTML - **Then** the Markdown output contains `## Item 1: Business`, `## Item 1A: Risk Factors`, and `### Market Competition` in that order Category: Illustrative Origin: PO #### S-conv-02: Filings with styled-p headings still produce heading hierarchy > Verifies end-to-end that the preprocessor preserves enough structural signal for the converter to identify headings in older filings - **Given** a filing where section headers are `

ITEM 1. BUSINESS

` with no h1-h6 tags - **When** the full pipeline runs (preprocess → convert) - **Then** the Markdown output has "ITEM 1. BUSINESS" as a heading (ATX format), not as a plain paragraph - **And** the document has at least one `#`/`##` heading in the output Category: Illustrative Origin: Multiple (Dev: styled-p headings, QA: no-heading filings, QA: preprocessor/converter ordering conflict) ### Rule: Converter falls back gracefully at import-time and invocation-time #### S-conv-03: Converter fallback triggers correctly > Verifies that the pipeline falls back to markdownify under various failure conditions - **Given** `` - **When** a filing is processed through the conversion step - **Then** `` and the frontmatter records `converter: ` | failure_condition | expected_behavior | converter_used | notes | |-------------------|-------------------|----------------|-------| | html-to-markdown cannot be imported (linux-aarch64, no wheel) | markdownify used for all conversions from startup | markdownify | import-time fallback | | html-to-markdown raises exception on specific HTML input | markdownify used for that filing only | markdownify | invocation-time fallback | | html-to-markdown returns empty string or whitespace-only output | Detected as silent failure; markdownify used for that filing | markdownify | silent failure detection | > **Note**: Fallback triggers on exception, empty output, or whitespace-only output. Ratio-based size thresholds (e.g., output < X% of input) are intentionally excluded — HTML preprocessing legitimately strips large amounts of XBRL/style noise, so a small output relative to raw HTML does not indicate converter failure. Content quality (e.g., missing sections) is validated by UAT manual inspection, not by automated fallback. Category: Illustrative (table-driven) Origin: Multiple (Dev: import vs invocation, Dev: silent failure, QA: threshold definition, User: Q3 decision) ### Rule: Both converters produce ATX-style headings #### S-conv-04: Same input produces ATX headings from both adapters > Verifies that both html-to-markdown and markdownify produce `## Heading` format (not Setext `Heading\n------`) - **Given** a preprocessed HTML snippet containing `

Item 7: Management's Discussion

` - **When** the same HTML is converted by HtmlToMarkdownAdapter and by MarkdownifyAdapter separately - **Then** both outputs contain `## Item 7: Management's Discussion` (ATX format) - **And** neither output uses Setext-style headings Category: Illustrative Origin: Dev (converter normalization, contested and upgraded by User Q4 decision) --- ### Journey Scenarios #### J-conv-01: Full pipeline with mixed content produces structured Markdown > Proves the complete pipeline preserves document structure through all stages - **Given** a real 10-K filing HTML containing headings, paragraphs, financial tables, and XBRL inline tags - **When** the full pipeline runs: download → preprocess → convert → save - **Then** the saved Markdown file has YAML frontmatter with all required fields - **And** the Markdown body contains ATX headings reflecting the filing's section structure - **And** financial tables are present as Markdown tables - **And** no residual HTML tags or XBRL namespace tags remain in the body Category: Journey Origin: Multiple --- ## Feature: Filing Store & Output Contract ### Context LocalFilingStore persists ParsedFilings as .md files with YAML frontmatter at `data/sec_filings/{ticker}/{filing_type}/{fiscal_year}.md`. Store operations must be consistent, handle real-world filesystem conditions, and produce correctly typed metadata. ### Rule: Store operations are mutually consistent #### S-store-01: save/exists/get/list_filings are consistent with each other > Verifies that all four store operations reflect the same state - **Given** `` - **When** `` is called - **Then** `` | precondition | operation | expected_result | notes | |--------------|-----------|-----------------|-------| | TSLA 10-K FY2024 saved | `exists("TSLA", FilingType.TEN_K, 2024)` | `True` | post-save existence | | TSLA 10-K FY2024 saved | `get("TSLA", FilingType.TEN_K, 2024)` | Returns ParsedFiling with valid frontmatter | post-save retrieval | | No TSLA FY2023 saved | `exists("TSLA", FilingType.TEN_K, 2023)` | `False` | non-existent filing | | No TSLA FY2023 saved | `get("TSLA", FilingType.TEN_K, 2023)` | `None` | non-existent filing | | NVDA FY2024, 2023, 2022 saved | `list_filings("NVDA", FilingType.TEN_K)` | Returns `[2024, 2023, 2022]` | list all fiscal years | | No MSFT filings saved | `list_filings("MSFT", FilingType.TEN_K)` | Returns `[]` | empty list | Category: Illustrative (table-driven) Origin: PO ### Rule: Store creates directories automatically on first save #### S-store-02: First save for a new ticker creates the directory structure > Verifies that save() creates intermediate directories when they don't exist - **Given** `data/sec_filings/TSLA/` directory does not exist - **When** `save()` is called for TSLA 10-K FY2024 - **Then** the directory `data/sec_filings/TSLA/10-K/` is created automatically - **And** the file `data/sec_filings/TSLA/10-K/2024.md` is written successfully Category: Illustrative Origin: Dev (directory auto-creation) ### Rule: list_filings filters non-filing files #### S-store-03: Non-filing files in the directory are excluded from list results > Verifies that filesystem artifacts don't pollute list_filings results - **Given** `data/sec_filings/NVDA/10-K/` contains `2024.md`, `2023.md`, `.DS_Store`, and `2024.md.tmp` - **When** `list_filings("NVDA", FilingType.TEN_K)` is called - **Then** the result is `[2024, 2023]` — only valid `{year}.md` files are included Category: Illustrative Origin: QA (.DS_Store), QA (temp file orphans) ### Rule: ParsedFiling metadata roundtrips correctly through save/get #### S-store-04: Frontmatter with special characters survives save/get roundtrip > Verifies that company names with YAML-problematic characters are serialized and deserialized correctly - **Given** a ParsedFiling for `` with company_name `` is saved - **When** `store.get("", FilingType.TEN_K, )` is called - **Then** the returned ParsedFiling has `company_name` equal to `` (exact match) | ticker | company_name | year | yaml_issue | |--------|-------------|------|------------| | MCO | Moody's Corporation | 2024 | apostrophe | | T | AT&T Inc. | 2024 | ampersand | | TROW | T. Rowe Price Group, Inc. | 2024 | comma and periods | Category: Illustrative (table-driven) Origin: Multiple (QA: YAML special chars, Dev: YAML serializer roundtrip) #### S-store-05: All required metadata fields are present and correctly typed > Verifies that a saved ParsedFiling has the complete frontmatter contract - **Given** NVDA 10-K FY2024 has been processed and saved - **When** `store.get("NVDA", FilingType.TEN_K, 2024)` is called - **Then** the frontmatter contains all required fields: - `ticker`: "NVDA" (string, uppercase) - `cik`: string (e.g., "1045810") - `company_name`: string (e.g., "NVIDIA Corporation") - `filing_type`: "10-K" (string) - `filing_date`: ISO date string - `fiscal_year`: 2024 (integer) - `accession_number`: string in dashed format (e.g., "0001045810-24-000029") - `source_url`: valid URL string - `parsed_at`: ISO 8601 UTC datetime with timezone (e.g., "2026-04-03T10:30:00Z") - `converter`: "html-to-markdown" or "markdownify" (string) Category: Illustrative Origin: PO --- ### Journey Scenarios #### J-store-01: Multi-ticker filing lifecycle through the store > Proves store operations work correctly across multiple tickers and fiscal years - **Given** a fresh (empty) filing store - **When** filings are saved for NVDA FY2024, NVDA FY2023, and AAPL FY2024 - **And** `list_filings("NVDA", FilingType.TEN_K)` is called - **And** `get("NVDA", FilingType.TEN_K, 2024)` is called - **And** `get("AAPL", FilingType.TEN_K, 2024)` is called - **Then** list_filings returns `[2024, 2023]` for NVDA - **And** both get() calls return ParsedFilings with correct ticker-specific metadata - **And** NVDA's filing is distinct from AAPL's (different ticker, cik, company_name) Category: Journey Origin: Multiple

1 # Verification Plan 2 3 ## Meta 4 5 - Scenarios Reference: `.artifacts/current/bdd-scenarios.md` 6 - Generated: 2026-04-03 7 8 --- 9 10 ## Automated Verification 11 12 ### Deterministic 13 14 > This is a backend data pipeline with no UI. All scenarios use script-based deterministic verification. Callers are batch scripts and agent tools — verification exercises the Python API directly. 15 16 #### S-dl-01: Cache determines whether SEC is contacted 17 - **Method**: script 18 - **Steps**: 19 1. Setup: ensure `data/sec_filings/NVDA/10-K/2024.md` exists (pre-saved fixture) 20 2. Call `process("NVDA", "10-K", fiscal_year=2024)` — assert it returns ParsedFiling without triggering download (mock or spy on SECDownloader) 21 3. Delete `data/sec_filings/AAPL/10-K/2023.md` if exists 22 4. Call `process("AAPL", "10-K", fiscal_year=2023)` — assert SECDownloader was called, file now exists at expected path 23 5. Call `process("NVDA", "10-K")` (no fiscal_year) — assert edgartools was contacted to resolve year, then cache was checked 24 - **Expected**: Cache hit skips download; cache miss triggers full pipeline; omitted fiscal_year contacts SEC for resolution before cache check 25 26 #### S-dl-02: Batch processes multiple tickers with mixed outcomes 27 - **Method**: script 28 - **Steps**: 29 1. Pre-cache NVDA 10-K FY2024 30 2. Ensure AAPL FY2023 is not cached 31 3. Call `process_batch(["NVDA", "AAPL", "FAKECORP"], "10-K")` 32 4. Assert result is `Dict[str, Result]` with exactly 3 keys 33 5. Assert `result["NVDA"].status == "success"` and `result["NVDA"].from_cache == True` 34 6. Assert `result["AAPL"].status == "success"` and `result["AAPL"].filing` is a valid ParsedFiling 35 7. Assert `result["FAKECORP"].status == "error"` and error message contains "FAKECORP" 36 8. Verify `data/sec_filings/AAPL/10-K/2023.md` was created 37 - **Expected**: Per-ticker results with clear status; successful downloads preserved despite FAKECORP failure 38 39 #### S-dl-03: Non-calendar fiscal year companies store with correct fiscal_year 40 - **Method**: script 41 - **Steps**: 42 1. Call `process("NVDA", "10-K")` — let it resolve and download the latest 10-K 43 2. Read the saved file's frontmatter: `store.get("NVDA", FilingType.TEN_K, [POST-CODING: determine NVDA's latest fiscal_year from edgartools])` 44 3. Assert `fiscal_year` in frontmatter matches edgartools' fiscal year for NVDA (company convention, not calendar year) 45 4. Assert the storage path uses the same fiscal year value 46 5. Repeat for MSFT to cover a different fiscal year end (June) 47 - **Expected**: fiscal_year in path and metadata aligns with edgartools/company convention for non-calendar FY companies 48 49 #### S-dl-04: "Latest" returns most recently filed 10-K 50 - **Method**: script 51 - **Steps**: 52 1. Call `process("NVDA", "10-K")` without fiscal_year 53 2. Separately query edgartools for NVDA's latest 10-K filing to get the expected fiscal year 54 3. Assert the returned ParsedFiling's `fiscal_year` matches the edgartools query result 55 4. Assert the filing exists at the expected path `data/sec_filings/NVDA/10-K/{expected_fy}.md` 56 - **Expected**: Omitting fiscal_year returns the most recently filed 10-K per edgartools 57 58 #### S-dl-05: Invalid inputs produce distinct, actionable error types 59 - **Method**: script 60 - **Steps**: 61 1. Call `process("NVDAA", "10-K")` — assert raises `[POST-CODING: exact error class name]` with "NVDAA" in message 62 2. Call `process("TSM", "10-K")` — assert raises a different error class indicating no 10-K filings (not "ticker not found") 63 3. Create ParsedFiling objects for: MCO (Moody's Corporation), T (AT&T Inc.), TROW (T. Rowe Price Group, Inc.) 2. Save each via `store.save()` 3. Read back each via `store.get()` 4. Assert `cik` is a string (not int), preserving format from edgartools - **Expected**: All metadata fields present, correctly typed, and following the contract --- ### Deterministic — Journey Scenarios #### J-dl-01: Batch pre-load followed by cache-only re-run - **Method**: script - **Steps**: 1. Process NVDA 10-K FY2024 normally — assert file saved 2. `[POST-CODING: manually corrupt the saved .md file — replace body with empty string or garbage HTML]` 3. Call `process("NVDA", "10-K", fiscal_year=2024)` (without force) — assert returns the corrupted content (cache hit) 4. Call `process("NVDA", "10-K", fiscal_year=2024, force=True)` — assert returns valid ParsedFiling 5. Assert `parsed_at` has been updated (more recent than original) 6. Assert Markdown body is non-empty and contains heading structure - **Expected**: Without force, bad cache is served; with force, filing is re-processed and corrected #### J-prep-01: Real filing HTML through full preprocess produces intact content - **Method**: script - **Steps**: 1. Download a real 10-K HTML for NVDA FY2024 via edgartools 2. Record the visible text length of the raw HTML (strip tags, count chars) 3. Pass through `HTMLPreprocessor.preprocess()` 4. Record the visible text length of preprocessed HTML 5. Assert preprocessed text length is at least 80% of original (no massive content loss) 6. Assert no ``, ``, ` 10KB (a real 10-K should produce substantial Markdown) - **Expected**: Full pipeline produces rich, structured Markdown with headings, tables, and clean text #### J-store-01: Multi-ticker filing lifecycle through the store - **Method**: script - **Steps**: 1. Create fresh LocalFilingStore 2. Process and save NVDA FY2024, NVDA FY2023, AAPL FY2024 3. Assert `list_filings("NVDA", FilingType.TEN_K)` returns `[2024, 2023]` 4. Assert `list_filings("AAPL", FilingType.TEN_K)` returns `[2024]` 5. Get NVDA FY2024 and AAPL FY2024 — assert different `ticker`, `cik`, `company_name` 6. Get NVDA FY2024 and NVDA FY2023 — assert same `ticker` but different `fiscal_year`, `filing_date` - **Expected**: Store correctly isolates filings by ticker and fiscal year; metadata is ticker-specific --- ## Manual Verification ### Manual Behavior Test > Tests requiring real SEC network calls or conditions that cannot be reliably mocked. #### S-dl-07: Overlapping writes to same filing (with real SEC calls) - **Reason**: True concurrency with real network I/O is difficult to mock reliably; real SEC latency creates the actual race window - **Steps**: 1. After both complete, inspect `data/sec_filings/AAPL/10-K/2024.md` 5. Verify the file has valid YAML frontmatter and non-empty Markdown body - **Expected**: File is valid regardless of which process "won" the write #### S-dl-05: Invalid inputs produce distinct errors (with real SEC) - **Reason**: Error type distinction for permanent errors is testable against real SEC; confirms edgartools' error propagation end-to-end - **Steps**: 1. Call `process("DEFINITELYNOTAREALTICKER12345", "10-K")` — expect "ticker not found" error 2. Call `process("TSM", "10-K")` — expect "no 10-K filings" error (distinct from ticker not found) 3. Verify both produce clear, distinct, actionable error messages - **Expected**: Real SEC permanent errors produce distinguishable error types ### User Acceptance Test > User validates that the pipeline's output meets requirements for downstream RAG use. #### J-conv-01: Full pipeline output quality check - **Acceptance Question**: Does the parsed Markdown faithfully represent the 10-K filing content in a RAG-friendly format? - **Steps**: 1. Process 3-5 real tickers (NVDA, AAPL, MSFT) for their latest 10-K 2. Open each `.md` file in a Markdown viewer (e.g., VS Code preview, Obsidian) 3. Verify: heading hierarchy matches the original 10-K sections (Item 1, 1A, 7, 7A, 8) 4. Verify: financial tables are readable (numbers aligned, columns distinguishable) 5. Verify: no residual HTML tags, XBRL artifacts, or garbled text 6. Verify: frontmatter metadata matches SEC filing details 7. Compare output from html-to-markdown vs markdownify for one filing: are headings consistent (both ATX)? - **Expected**: Clean, structured Markdown that a human can read and a MarkdownNodeParser can chunk into meaningful sections #### S-prep-03 / S-conv-02: Older filing preprocessing quality - **Acceptance Question**: Does the pipeline handle older SEC filings (pre-2015) without destroying content? - **Steps**: 1. Process 2-3 older filings (e.g., AAPL FY2010, GE FY2008) — filings likely to use `` tags instead of semantic headings 2. Open the `.md` files and inspect 3. Verify: content is present (not empty or near-empty) 4. Verify: some heading structure exists (even if imperfect for very old filings) 5. 1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import logging
4	import time
5	from dataclasses import dataclass
6	from datetime import UTC, datetime
7	from typing import Literal
8	
9	from backend.ingestion.sec_filing_pipeline.filing_models import (
10	    FilingMetadata,
11	    FilingType,
12	    ParsedFiling,
13	    SECPipelineError,
14	    TransientError,
15	    UnsupportedFilingTypeError,
16	)
17	from backend.ingestion.sec_filing_pipeline.filing_store import (
18	    FilingStore,
19	    LocalFilingStore,
20	)
21	from backend.ingestion.sec_filing_pipeline.html_preprocessor import HTMLPreprocessor
22	from backend.ingestion.sec_filing_pipeline.html_to_md_converter import (
23	    HTMLToMarkdownConverter,
24	    HtmlToMarkdownAdapter,
25	    MarkdownifyAdapter,
26	    convert_with_fallback,
27	)
28	from backend.ingestion.sec_filing_pipeline.sec_downloader import SECDownloader
29	
30	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
31	
32	_MAX_BATCH_RETRIES = 3
33	_RETRY_BASE_DELAY = 1.0
34	
35	
36	@dataclass(frozen=True)
37	class BatchResult:
38	    status: Literal["success", "error"]
39	    filing: ParsedFiling | None
40	    error: str | None
41	    from_cache: bool
42	
43	
44	class SECFilingPipeline:
45	    def __init__(
46	        self,
47	        downloader: SECDownloader,
48	        preprocessor: HTMLPreprocessor,
49	        converter: HTMLToMarkdownConverter,
50	        fallback_converter: HTMLToMarkdownConverter,
51	        store: FilingStore,
52	    ) -> None:
53	        self._downloader = downloader
54	        self._preprocessor = preprocessor
55	        self._converter = converter
56	        self._fallback_converter = fallback_converter
57	        self._store = store
58	
59	    @classmethod
60	    def create(cls) -> SECFilingPipeline:
61	        return cls(
62	            downloader=SECDownloader(),
63	            preprocessor=HTMLPreprocessor(),
64	            converter=HtmlToMarkdownAdapter(),
65	            fallback_converter=MarkdownifyAdapter(),
66	            store=LocalFilingStore(),
67	        )
68	
69	    def process(
70	        self,
71	        ticker: str,
72	        filing_type: str,
73	        fiscal_year: int | None = None,
74	        force: bool = False,
75	    ) -> ParsedFiling:
76	        filing, _ = self._process_internal(ticker, filing_type, fiscal_year, force)
77	        return filing
78	
79	    def _process_internal(
80	        self,
81	        ticker: str,
82	        filing_type: str,
83	        fiscal_year: int | None = None,
84	        force: bool = False,
85	    ) -> tuple[ParsedFiling, bool]:
86	        ticker = ticker.upper()
87	        self._validate_filing_type(filing_type)
88	
89	        if fiscal_year is not None and not force:
90	            cached = self._store.get(ticker, FilingType(filing_type), fiscal_year)
91	            if cached is not None:
92	                return cached, True
93	
94	        raw = self._downloader.download(ticker, filing_type, fiscal_year)
95	
96	        if fiscal_year is None and not force:
97	            cached = self._store.get(ticker, FilingType(filing_type), raw.fiscal_year)
98	            if cached is not None:
99	                return cached, True
100	
101	        cleaned_html = self._preprocessor.preprocess(raw.raw_html)
102	        markdown, converter_name = convert_with_fallback(
103	            cleaned_html, self._converter, self._fallback_converter
104	        )
105	
106	        metadata = FilingMetadata(
107	            ticker=raw.ticker,
108	            cik=raw.cik,
109	            company_name=raw.company_name,
110	            filing_type=FilingType(filing_type),
111	            filing_date=raw.filing_date,
112	            fiscal_year=raw.fiscal_year,
113	            accession_number=raw.accession_number,
114	            source_url=raw.source_url,
115	            parsed_at=datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(),
116	            converter=converter_name,
117	        )
118	        filing = ParsedFiling(metadata=metadata, markdown_content=markdown)
119	        self._store.save(filing)
120	        return filing, False
121	
122	    def process_batch(
123	        self,
124	        tickers: list[str],
125	        filing_type: str,
126	    ) -> dict[str, BatchResult]:
127	        results: dict[str, BatchResult] = {}
128	        for ticker in tickers:
129	            normalized = ticker.upper()
130	            results[normalized] = self._process_with_retry(normalized, filing_type)
131	        return results
132	
133	    def _process_with_retry(self, ticker: str, filing_type: str) -> BatchResult:
134	        last_error: Exception | None = None
135	        for attempt in range(_MAX_BATCH_RETRIES):
136	            try:
137	                filing, from_cache = self._process_internal(ticker, filing_type)
138	                return BatchResult(
139	                    status="success", filing=filing, error=None, from_cache=from_cache
140	                )
141	            except TransientError as exc:
142	                last_error = exc
143	                if attempt < _MAX_BATCH_RETRIES - 1:
144	                    delay = _RETRY_BASE_DELAY * (2**attempt)
145	                    logger.warning(
146	                        "Transient error for %s (attempt %d/%d), retrying in %.1fs: %s",
147	                        ticker,
148	                        attempt + 1,
149	                        _MAX_BATCH_RETRIES,
150	                        delay,
151	                        exc,
152	                    )
153	                    time.sleep(delay)
154	            except SECPipelineError as exc:
155	                return BatchResult(
156	                    status="error", filing=None, error=str(exc), from_cache=False
157	                )
158	
159	        return BatchResult(
160	            status="error", filing=None, error=str(last_error), from_cache=False
161	        )
162	
163	    @staticmethod
164	    def _validate_filing_type(filing_type: str) -> None:
165	        if filing_type not in FilingType.__members__.values():
166	            raise UnsupportedFilingTypeError(f"Unsupported filing type: {filing_type}")
167

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import os
4	
5	from edgar import Company, CompanyNotFoundError, set_identity
6	
7	from backend.ingestion.sec_filing_pipeline.filing_models import (
8	    FilingNotFoundError,
9	    FilingType,
10	    RawFiling,
11	    TickerNotFoundError,
12	    UnsupportedFilingTypeError,
13	)
14	
15	
16	class SECDownloader:
17	    def __init__(self) -> None:
18	        identity = os.environ.get("EDGAR_IDENTITY")
19	        if identity:
20	            set_identity(identity)
21	
22	    def download(
23	        self,
24	        ticker: str,
25	        filing_type: str,
26	        fiscal_year: int | None = None,
27	    ) -> RawFiling:
28	        ticker = ticker.upper()
29	
30	        if filing_type not in FilingType.__members__.values():
31	            raise UnsupportedFilingTypeError(f"Unsupported filing type: {filing_type}")
32	
33	        try:
34	            company = Company(ticker)
35	        except CompanyNotFoundError as exc:
36	            raise TickerNotFoundError(f"Ticker not found: {ticker}") from exc
37	
38	        filings = company.get_filings(form=filing_type)
39	
40	        if fiscal_year is not None:
41	            filings = filings.filter(
42	                date=f"{fiscal_year - 1}-01-01:{fiscal_year + 1}-01-01"
43	            )
44	
45	        filing = filings.latest()
46	
47	        if filing is None:
48	            raise FilingNotFoundError(
49	                f"No {filing_type} filing found for {ticker}"
50	                + (f" (fiscal year {fiscal_year})" if fiscal_year else "")
51	            )
52	
53	        derived_fy = int(str(filing.period_of_report)[:4])
54	
55	        if fiscal_year is not None and derived_fy != fiscal_year:
56	            raise FilingNotFoundError(
57	                f"No {filing_type} filing found for {ticker}"
58	                f" matching fiscal year {fiscal_year}"
59	                f" (closest match: {derived_fy})"
60	            )
61	
62	        return RawFiling(
63	            raw_html=filing.html(),
64	            ticker=ticker,
65	            cik=str(company.cik),
66	            company_name=company.name,
67	            filing_date=str(filing.filing_date),
68	            fiscal_year=derived_fy,
69	            accession_number=filing.accession_number,
70	            source_url=filing.filing_url,
71	        )
72

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import os
4	import tempfile
5	from pathlib import Path
6	from typing import Protocol, runtime_checkable
7	
8	import yaml
9	
10	from backend.ingestion.sec_filing_pipeline.filing_models import (
11	    FilingMetadata,
12	    FilingType,
13	    ParsedFiling,
14	)
15	
16	FRONTMATTER_DELIMITER = "---"
17	
18	
19	@runtime_checkable
20	class FilingStore(Protocol):
21	    def save(self, filing: ParsedFiling) -> None: ...
22	
23	    def get(
24	        self, ticker: str, filing_type: FilingType, fiscal_year: int
25	    ) -> ParsedFiling | None: ...
26	
27	    def exists(
28	        self, ticker: str, filing_type: FilingType, fiscal_year: int
29	    ) -> bool: ...
30	
31	    def list_filings(self, ticker: str, filing_type: FilingType) -> list[int]: ...
32	
33	
34	class LocalFilingStore:
35	    def __init__(self, base_dir: str = "data/sec_filings") -> None:
36	        self._base_dir = Path(base_dir)
37	
38	    def _filing_dir(self, ticker: str, filing_type: FilingType) -> Path:
39	        return self._base_dir / ticker.upper() / str(filing_type)
40	
41	    def _filing_path(
42	        self, ticker: str, filing_type: FilingType, fiscal_year: int
43	    ) -> Path:
44	        return self._filing_dir(ticker, filing_type) / f"{fiscal_year}.md"
45	
46	    def save(self, filing: ParsedFiling) -> None:
47	        ticker = filing.metadata.ticker.upper()
48	        path = self._filing_path(
49	            ticker, filing.metadata.filing_type, filing.metadata.fiscal_year
50	        )
51	        os.makedirs(path.parent, exist_ok=True)
52	
53	        meta_dict = filing.metadata.model_dump()
54	        meta_dict["ticker"] = ticker
55	        meta_dict["filing_type"] = str(filing.metadata.filing_type)
56	
57	        frontmatter = yaml.dump(
58	            meta_dict, default_flow_style=False, allow_unicode=True, sort_keys=False
59	        )
60	        content = (
61	            f"{FRONTMATTER_DELIMITER}\n"
62	            f"{frontmatter}"
63	            f"{FRONTMATTER_DELIMITER}\n\n"
64	            f"{filing.markdown_content}"
65	        )
66	
67	        fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(
68	            dir=path.parent, suffix=".tmp", prefix=".filing_"
69	        )
70	        try:
71	            with os.fdopen(fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
72	                f.write(content)
73	            os.replace(tmp_path, path)
74	        except BaseException:
75	            if os.path.exists(tmp_path):
76	                os.unlink(tmp_path)
77	            raise
78	
79	    def get(
80	        self, ticker: str, filing_type: FilingType, fiscal_year: int
81	    ) -> ParsedFiling | None:
82	        path = self._filing_path(ticker, filing_type, fiscal_year)
83	        if not path.exists():
84	            return None
85	
86	        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
87	        metadata, markdown_content = _parse_frontmatter(text)
88	        return ParsedFiling(metadata=metadata, markdown_content=markdown_content)
89	
90	    def exists(self, ticker: str, filing_type: FilingType, fiscal_year: int) -> bool:
91	        return self._filing_path(ticker, filing_type, fiscal_year).exists()
92	
93	    def list_filings(self, ticker: str, filing_type: FilingType) -> list[int]:
94	        directory = self._filing_dir(ticker, filing_type)
95	        if not directory.exists():
96	            return []
97	
98	        years: list[int] = []
99	        for entry in directory.iterdir():
100	            if entry.suffix != ".md":
101	                continue
102	            try:
103	                years.append(int(entry.stem))
104	            except ValueError:
105	                continue
106	        return sorted(years)
107	
108	
109	def _parse_frontmatter(text: str) -> tuple[FilingMetadata, str]:
110	    stripped = text.lstrip("\n")
111	    if not stripped.startswith(FRONTMATTER_DELIMITER):
112	        msg = "Missing YAML frontmatter delimiter"
113	        raise ValueError(msg)
114	
115	    after_first = stripped[len(FRONTMATTER_DELIMITER) :].lstrip("\n")
116	    end_idx = after_first.index(FRONTMATTER_DELIMITER)
117	    yaml_block = after_first[:end_idx]
118	    body = after_first[end_idx + len(FRONTMATTER_DELIMITER) :].lstrip("\n")
119	
120	    meta_dict = yaml.safe_load(yaml_block)
121	    metadata = FilingMetadata(**meta_dict)
122	    return metadata, body
123

1	import re
2	
3	from bs4 import BeautifulSoup, Tag
4	
5	_PART_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*PART\s+(I{1,3}V?|IV)\b", re.IGNORECASE)
6	_ITEM_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*Item\s+\d+[A-Z]?\.", re.IGNORECASE)
7	
8	_DECORATIVE_PROPS = frozenset(
9	    {
10	        "font-family",
11	        "font-size",
12	        "font-style",
13	        "color",
14	        "background-color",
15	        "background",
16	        "padding",
17	        "padding-top",
18	        "padding-bottom",
19	        "padding-left",
20	        "padding-right",
21	        "margin",
22	        "margin-top",
23	        "margin-bottom",
24	        "margin-left",
25	        "margin-right",
26	        "border",
27	        "border-top",
28	        "border-bottom",
29	        "border-left",
30	        "border-right",
31	        "line-height",
32	        "letter-spacing",
33	        "text-decoration",
34	        "text-indent",
35	        "vertical-align",
36	        "width",
37	        "height",
38	        "min-width",
39	        "min-height",
40	        "max-width",
41	        "max-height",
42	    }
43	)
44	
45	_BLOCK_TAGS = frozenset({"div", "p", "td", "th", "span"})
46	
47	
48	def _has_bold_signal(tag: Tag) -> bool:
49	    if tag.find(["b", "strong"]):
50	        return True
51	    style = tag.get("style", "")
52	    if isinstance(style, list):
53	        style = ";".join(style)
54	    if re.search(r"font-weight\s*:\s*(700|bold)", style):
55	        return True
56	    for desc in tag.descendants:
57	        if isinstance(desc, Tag):
58	            desc_style = desc.get("style", "")
59	            if isinstance(desc_style, list):
60	                desc_style = ";".join(desc_style)
61	            if re.search(r"font-weight\s*:\s*(700|bold)", desc_style):
62	                return True
63	    return False
64	
65	
66	def _parse_style(style_str: str) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
67	    props = []
68	    for part in style_str.split(";"):
69	        part = part.strip()
70	        if ":" not in part:
71	            continue
72	        name, _, value = part.partition(":")
73	        props.append((name.strip().lower(), value.strip()))
74	    return props
75	
76	
77	def _filter_decorative_styles(style_str: str) -> str | None:
78	    props = _parse_style(style_str)
79	    kept = [f"{name}:{value}" for name, value in props if name not in _DECORATIVE_PROPS]
80	    return "; ".join(kept) if kept else None
81	
82	
83	class HTMLPreprocessor:
84	    def preprocess(self, html: str) -> str:
85	        soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
86	        self._strip_xbrl_tags(soup)
87	        self._remove_hidden_elements(soup)
88	        self._strip_decorative_styles(soup)
89	        self._unwrap_font_tags(soup)
90	        self._promote_headings(soup)
91	        return str(soup)
92	
93	    def _strip_xbrl_tags(self, soup: BeautifulSoup) -> None:
94	        for tag in soup.find_all(re.compile(r"^ix:")):
95	            tag.unwrap()
96	
97	    def _remove_hidden_elements(self, soup: BeautifulSoup) -> None:
98	        for tag in soup.find_all(
99	            style=re.compile(r"display\s*:\s*none", re.IGNORECASE)
100	        ):
101	            tag.decompose()
102	
103	    def _strip_decorative_styles(self, soup: BeautifulSoup) -> None:
104	        for tag in soup.find_all(style=True):
105	            style_str = tag["style"]
106	            if isinstance(style_str, list):
107	                style_str = ";".join(style_str)
108	            filtered = _filter_decorative_styles(style_str)
109	            if filtered:
110	                tag["style"] = filtered
111	            else:
112	                del tag["style"]
113	
114	    def _unwrap_font_tags(self, soup: BeautifulSoup) -> None:
115	        for font_tag in soup.find_all("font"):
116	            font_tag.unwrap()
117	
118	    def _promote_headings(self, soup: BeautifulSoup) -> None:
119	        promoted = set()
120	        for tag in reversed(soup.find_all(_BLOCK_TAGS)):
121	            if id(tag) in promoted:
122	                continue
123	            if any(id(d) in promoted for d in tag.descendants if isinstance(d, Tag)):
124	                continue
125	
126	            text = tag.get_text(strip=True)
127	            if not text:
128	                continue
129	
130	            heading_level = None
131	            if _PART_RE.match(text):
132	                heading_level = "h1"
133	            elif _ITEM_RE.match(text):
134	                heading_level = "h2"
135	
136	            if not heading_level or not _has_bold_signal(tag):
137	                continue
138	
139	            if any(
140	                child.name in _BLOCK_TAGS and child.get_text(strip=True)
141	                for child in tag.children
142	                if isinstance(child, Tag)
143	            ):
144	                continue
145	
146	            promoted.add(id(tag))
147	            tag.name = heading_level
148	            tag.string = text
149	            if tag.attrs:
150	                tag.attrs.clear()
151

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import logging
4	from typing import Protocol
5	
6	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
7	
8	
9	class HTMLToMarkdownConverter(Protocol):
10	    @property
11	    def name(self) -> str: ...
12	    def convert(self, html: str) -> str: ...
13	
14	
15	class HtmlToMarkdownAdapter:
16	    @property
17	    def name(self) -> str:
18	        return "html-to-markdown"
19	
20	    def convert(self, html: str) -> str:
21	        from html_to_markdown import ConversionOptions
22	        from html_to_markdown import convert as htm_convert
23	
24	        result = htm_convert(html, options=ConversionOptions(heading_style="atx"))
25	        return result["content"] or ""
26	
27	
28	class MarkdownifyAdapter:
29	    @property
30	    def name(self) -> str:
31	        return "markdownify"
32	
33	    def convert(self, html: str) -> str:
34	        import markdownify
35	
36	        return markdownify.markdownify(html, heading_style=markdownify.ATX)
37	
38	
39	def create_converter() -> HTMLToMarkdownConverter:
40	    try:
41	        import html_to_markdown as _  # noqa: F811, F401
42	
43	        return HtmlToMarkdownAdapter()
44	    except ImportError:
45	        logger.warning("html-to-markdown not available, falling back to markdownify")
46	        return MarkdownifyAdapter()
47	
48	
49	def convert_with_fallback(
50	    html: str,
51	    primary: HTMLToMarkdownConverter,
52	    fallback: HTMLToMarkdownConverter,
53	) -> tuple[str, str]:
54	    try:
55	        md = primary.convert(html)
56	    except Exception:
57	        logger.warning(
58	            "Primary converter '%s' failed, falling back to '%s'",
59	            primary.name,
60	            fallback.name,
61	        )
62	        md = fallback.convert(html)
63	        return md, fallback.name
64	
65	    if not md or len(md) < len(html) * 0.01:
66	        logger.warning(
67	            "Primary converter '%s' produced suspiciously small output "
68	            "(%d bytes from %d bytes input), falling back to '%s'",
69	            primary.name,
70	            len(md) if md else 0,
71	            len(html),
72	            fallback.name,
73	        )
74	        md = fallback.convert(html)
75	        return md, fallback.name
76	
77	    return md, primary.name
78

391	def _build_pipeline(tmp_path):
392	    store = LocalFilingStore(base_dir=str(tmp_path))
393	    return SECFilingPipeline(
394	        downloader=SECDownloader(),
395	        preprocessor=HTMLPreprocessor(),
396	        converter=HtmlToMarkdownAdapter(),
397	        fallback_converter=MarkdownifyAdapter(),
398	        store=store,

412	    def test_j_dl_01_batch_preload_and_cache(self):
413	        """Batch pre-load 3 tickers; re-run returns all from cache."""
414	        tickers = ["NVDA", "AAPL", "TSLA"]
415	        first_run = self.pipeline.process_batch(tickers, "10-K")
416	
417	        for t in tickers:
418	            assert first_run[t].status == "success"
419	            assert first_run[t].from_cache is False
420	
421	        first_parsed_at = {t: first_run[t].filing.metadata.parsed_at for t in tickers}
422	
423	        second_run = self.pipeline.process_batch(tickers, "10-K")
424	
425	        for t in tickers:
426	            assert second_run[t].status == "success"
427	            assert second_run[t].from_cache is True
428	            assert second_run[t].filing.metadata.parsed_at == first_parsed_at[t]
429	
430	    def test_j_dl_02_jit_cache_miss_then_hit(self):
431	        """JIT cache miss triggers download; follow-up is a cache hit."""
432	        filing1 = self.pipeline.process("NVDA", "10-K")
433	        assert filing1.metadata.ticker == "NVDA"
434	        fy = filing1.metadata.fiscal_year
435	
436	        filing2 = self.pipeline.process("NVDA", "10-K", fiscal_year=fy)
437	        assert filing2.metadata.parsed_at == filing1.metadata.parsed_at
438	
439	        filing3 = self.pipeline.process("NVDA", "10-K")
440	        assert filing3.metadata.parsed_at == filing1.metadata.parsed_at
441	
442	    def test_j_dl_03_force_reprocess_corrects_cached(self):
443	        """force=True re-downloads even when a cached (corrupt) file exists."""
444	        filing = self.pipeline.process("NVDA", "10-K")
445	        fy = filing.metadata.fiscal_year
446	
447	        md_path = self.tmp_path / "NVDA" / "10-K" / f"{fy}.md"
448	        raw_text = md_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
449	        parts = raw_text.split("---", 2)
450	        corrupted = f"---{parts[1]}---\n\nBAD BODY"
451	        md_path.write_text(corrupted, encoding="utf-8")
452	
453	        corrupt = self.store.get("NVDA", FilingType.TEN_K, fy)
454	        assert corrupt.markdown_content == "BAD BODY"
455	
456	        cached_no_force = self.pipeline.process("NVDA", "10-K", fiscal_year=fy)
457	        assert cached_no_force.markdown_content == "BAD BODY"
458	
459	        refreshed = self.pipeline.process("NVDA", "10-K", fiscal_year=fy, force=True)
460	        assert refreshed.markdown_content != "BAD BODY"
461	        assert len(refreshed.markdown_content) > 1000
